keyPoints.description: Natrum carbonicum and Glonoinum suffer in summer – sunstroke, headache, heat aggravation.
Silicea and Hepar are very sensitive to cold, especially during winter.
Rhus tox and Dulcamara worsen during rainy, damp seasons.
Sepia and Medorrhinum feel better in dry, warm summer weather.
Psorinum suffers in both winter and spring – marked aggravation from cold, change of season.

rubrics.rubric: Craving to eat but gets full quickly
Appetite with constant desire to nibble
Appetite with craving for indigestible things (chalk, clay)
Hunger with trembling if not eating
Aversion to fat or oily food
Cannot sleep unless lying on abdomen
Lying crosswise or in strange postures
Worse from pressure of clothing on abdomen
Wants to be completely covered (even in heat)
Worse from weather change (hot to cold or vice versa)
Worse from exposure to cold dry wind
Sexual desire suppressed after grief or disappointment
Loss of semen with stool or urine
Sexual dreams causing weakness or prostration
Sexual suppression from guilt or religion
Menses flow only while lying down
Colic continues even after flow begins
Thirst for large quantities at long intervals
Urgent stool driving out of bed in morning
Chilly patient – generally worse from cold
Chilly but worse in closed room
Chilly and worse from slightest draft
Chilly and always wants to be covered
Hot patient – desires cool air and uncovering
Hot patient but sensitive to cold drinks
Hot patient, < sun or heat of stove
One hand cold, the other hot
Hot patient – generally worse from heat
Hot patient – better in open air
Hot patient – worse in sun or stove heat
Hot patient – one hand hot, one cold
Yawning with sleepiness but cannot sleep
Sleeps on back – hands over head

keyPoints.description: Sleeplessness without physical or emotional reason – consider Coffea, Nux-v, or Ignatia.
Child sleeps on abdomen – seen in Calc, Medorrhinum, Sulphur.
Dreams of falling often confirm Borax or Calcarea in nervous children.

rubrics.rubric: Desire for chalk, sand, slate, pencils
Desire for smoked or burned food (roasted, BBQ)

keyPoints.description: Children who crave chalk, slate, or pencils often point to Calcarea or Alumina.
Aversion to milk or problems after milk are classic in Sepia and Calcarea.
Desire for vinegar or pickles is confirmatory in Sepia and Nitric acid cases.
Strong craving for alcohol or beer – think of Nux-v, Sulphur, or Lachesis.
Pulsatilla dislikes fat and meat, while craving butter and pastry.
Desire for spicy food with burning – think of Nux-v and Arsenicum.

rubrics.rubric: Dreams – of exam failure or shame

keyPoints.description: Frightful or violent dreams often confirm Stramonium or Belladonna in sensitive minds.
Dreams of falling, especially at sleep onset, confirm Borax in children.
Flying or prophetic dreams suggest Phosphorus or Lachesis.
Snake dreams with fear, sexuality or mysticism point to Lachesis or Medorrhinum.
Dreams of accidents or disasters during anxiety states may require Aconite or Arsenicum.
Children who wet the bed with urination dreams may benefit from Causticum or Equisetum.
Violent, war-like dreams in disturbed sleep states often respond to Hyos, Stram, or Bell.
Dreams of exams, failure, or social shame suggest Silicea, Arg-n, or Lycopodium.
Ghost or spirit dreams can reflect Stramonium’s deep subconscious fears.
Excessive hunger with weight loss suggests Phosphorus or Iodum.
Loss of appetite with aversion to food smell – think of Sepia or Pulsatilla.
Variable appetite is often seen in China and Cyclamen cases.
Arsenicum drinks frequently but in small sips, especially during restlessness.
Pulsatilla typically has thirstlessness even in dry fevers.
Phosphorus craves cold water but may vomit when it warms in stomach.
China and Kali-c show frequent thirst but for small amounts of water.
Bryonia shows intense thirst for large quantities at long intervals.

rubrics.rubric: Constipation with no desire for stool
Diarrhoea in the morning, drives out of bed

keyPoints.description: Nux vomica is key for ineffectual urging and constant dissatisfaction after stool.
Sulphur and Aloe are best for early morning diarrhoea driving patient out of bed.
Opium and Alumina for constipation with complete inactivity of rectum.
China and Phosphorus: diarrhoea immediately after meals or drinks.
Silicea: constipation with straining and stool recedes back (bashful).
Silicea and Calcarea often sweat profusely at night, especially on head and neck.
Sweat from slightest exertion is characteristic of China and Phosphorus.
Rheum and Silicea have sour offensive sweat, especially on scalp or soles.
Thuja and Fluoric acid show sweat only on specific isolated parts.
Lack of sweat in high fever suggests Apis or Belladonna.

rubrics.rubric: Aversion to cold (wants warmth, cannot tolerate wind)

keyPoints.description: Psorinum and Silicea are classic chilly patients, worse from slightest cold.
Sulphur and Lachesis are hot-blooded, worse from heat and crowding.
Camphor and Secale show paradox – chilly but want uncovering.
Hepar and Nux vomica have extreme cold sensitivity and desire for warmth.
Phosphorus craves fresh air and is aggravated in stuffy rooms.

rubrics.rubric: Sleep position – on back with hands overhead

keyPoints.description: Coffea and Nux vomica have sleeplessness due to racing thoughts or excitement.
Arsenicum and Psorinum often wake after midnight with restlessness.
Medorrhinum and Kali carbonicum prefer knee-chest sleep position.
Sulphur, Phosphorus, and Calcarea lie on their back with arms overhead.
Gelsemium and Nux moschata show excessive daytime sleepiness with heaviness.

rubrics.rubric: Desire for sour things (pickles, vinegar, etc)
Desire for indigestible / unusual things (chalk, mud, etc)

keyPoints.description: Natrum muriaticum has strong craving for salt and sour, but aversion to bread and fat.
Phosphorus craves cold drinks, sweets, and spicy foods, but may dislike warm food.
Pulsatilla dislikes fatty and spicy food, craves sour and sweets but milk disagrees.
Sepia has aversion to meat, milk, and sweets but may crave vinegar or sour food.
Calcarea carbonica craves eggs, milk, and indigestible things like chalk.

rubrics.rubric: Unable to move due to pain (pain prevents motion)

keyPoints.description: Bryonia and Apis are worse from slightest motion – want total rest.
Rhus tox and Arsenicum feel better by moving constantly, despite fatigue.
Colocynth and Belladonna experience pain so intense it prevents any movement.
Zincum and Tarentula show restless fidgeting, cannot stay still.
Bryonia and Belladonna are worse lying on painful or affected side.
Colocynth is better lying on abdomen or bending forward due to colic pain.
Lachesis worsens on left side and lying on back, typical left-sided remedy.
Medorrhinum and Kali carbonicum prefer knee-chest posture during pain.
Sulphur aggravates from sitting or lying on back, prefers standing or walking.

rubrics.rubric: Aggravation from tight clothing (especially around waist/neck)
Aversion to clothing/touch on affected part
Wants to be uncovered / cannot tolerate clothes
Wants to be covered warmly despite heat

keyPoints.description: Lachesis cannot tolerate any tight clothing around neck or waist, feels suffocated.
Sulphur and Secale feel better when completely uncovered, even if chilly.
Arsenicum and Psorinum need to stay covered, even during fever or warmth.
Chamomilla and Apis can't tolerate even light touch on inflamed areas.
Carbo veg improves by loosening tight garments around chest and abdomen.
Sulphur aggravates from bathing generally, worse from both hot and cold water.
Silicea and Psorinum dislike bathing and may get sick after it.
Rhus tox improves from warm bathing, while cold bathing worsens pains.
Apis and Secale are better from cold bathing, especially in burning complaints.
Medorrhinum and Antimonium tart worsen from hot bathing due to skin reactivity.
Pulsatilla, Sepia, and Natrum mur are the leading remedies for late, scanty or suppressed menses.
Lachesis typically has dark, offensive, clotted menses that aggravate all complaints.
Belladonna and Sabina show profuse, bright red bleeding with cramps.
Mag phos and Chamomilla are useful in painful menses better from heat and pressure.
Medorrhinum and Psorinum are often used when menses are offensive, irregular, or suppress other conditions.
Pulsatilla is a classic remedy for fever without thirst, especially in children.
Arsenicum has fever with intense chilliness, restlessness, and thirst for sips.
Belladonna and Stramonium show high fever with delirium and red face.
China (Chin) is known for periodic fever with profuse sweating and weakness.
Eupatorium perfoliatum has fever with intense bone pains and chill before fever.

rubrics.rubric: Sweat only on upper part of body
Sweat only on lower part of body

keyPoints.description: Calcarea and Silicea are well known for head sweat during sleep, especially in children.
Mercurius has offensive, profuse sweat even from slight exertion.
Sulphur and Psorinum have strong body odor and offensive sweating patterns.
Chin and Phos-ac are key in debilitating, exhaustive sweating after acute illness.
Sweat pattern localization (upper/lower body) is key in remedy selection – e.g., Lach, Sep, Thuja.
Pulsatilla and Gelsemium are commonly indicated in chilliness without thirst, worse in closed room.
Nux vomica shows chilliness from uncovering, worse in open air, better by warmth.
Arsenicum chills often start with intense thirst, anxiety, and restlessness.
Eupatorium perfoliatum shows chills starting in the back with bone pain, followed by fever.
Hepar sulph and Silicea are very sensitive to draft and uncovering.

rubrics.rubric: Heat with desire for fanning or fresh air

keyPoints.description: Belladonna has intense heat with red face, throbbing pulse, and desire to uncover.
Sulphur and Apis have strong aggravation from heat and coverings.
Lachesis, Sepia, and Glonoinum are indicated in flushes of heat during menopause.
Arsenicum and Aconitum combine heat with anxiety, restlessness, and thirst for sips.
Pulsatilla has heat without thirst, wants fresh air and fanning.

rubrics.rubric: Itching, worse from heat or warmth

keyPoints.description: Sulphur, Graphites, and Psorinum are top remedies for intense itching, better by scratching but worse later.
Formication is a hallmark of Agaricus and Zincum with nervous origin.
Petroleum and Graphites are indicated for dry, cracked, oozing skin worse in winter.
Burning skin with restlessness and anxiety – think Arsenicum and Rhus tox.
Ant crudum and Urtica urens often cover summer-aggravated itching and rashes.

rubrics.rubric: Discharges – staining linen yellow or green

keyPoints.description: Mercurius and Kreosotum are known for acrid, offensive, greenish discharges.
Kali bichromicum produces stringy, ropy mucus from any orifice.
Calcarea and Pulsatilla cover bland, thick, white or milky discharges especially in children and females.
Hamamelis and Phosphorus suit bleeding discharges with bright blood.
Psorinum and Silicea are top for horribly offensive, long-standing discharges.

rubrics.rubric: Urine – very scanty and high colored

keyPoints.description: Benzoic acid and Nitric acid cover highly offensive, strong-smelling urine with sediment.
Lycopodium and Sarsaparilla are key for thick, white or red brick-dust sediment in urine.
Hamamelis and Terebinthina are classic for hematuria (bloody urine).
Sepia, Medorrhinum, and Merc cor suit fishy, putrid urine often linked with vaginal discharge or STIs.
Apis and Arsenicum are suited for dark, scanty, burning urine with restlessness.

rubrics.rubric: Craving – raw rice or uncooked grains
Craving – urine or to drink own urine

keyPoints.description: Calcarea and Alumina commonly show desire for chalk, pencils, or clay – classic pica symptoms in anemia.
Pica is a nosode and remedy name associated with strange cravings including soap, paper, rags.
Stramonium and Syphilinum cover darkest forms of cravings, such as for urine, feces, or filth.
Silicea, Cina, and Alumina also cover dirt/earth cravings, especially in children or women with nutrient deficiencies.

rubrics.rubric: Aggravation from farinaceous food (bread, rice, etc.)
Amelioration from sour things (pickles, vinegar, lemon)
Amelioration from cold food and drinks

keyPoints.description: Pulsatilla is aggravated by rich, fatty foods and meat, yet desires them.
Phosphorus and Veratrum album prefer cold drinks and ice cream which often relieve symptoms.
Arsenicum is worse from fruits and cold drinks, but better with warmth.
Natrum carbonicum and Calcarea are frequently aggravated by milk and eggs.
Lycopodium is better after warm drinks and food, and worse from beans or farinaceous food.

rubrics.rubric: Sleep – falls asleep late despite tiredness
Sleep – feels drowsy all day, sleepless at night
Sleep – talking or groaning in sleep

keyPoints.description: Sulphur has many sleep modalities – arms overhead, worse after midnight, talking in sleep, and waking at 2–3 a.m.
Nux vomica – trouble falling asleep, sleeps late despite fatigue, drowsy in day and sleepless at night.
Phosphorus prefers left side, has unrefreshing sleep, and may talk in sleep or wake late.
Stramonium and Belladonna are notable for night terrors, talking/groaning, and somnambulism.
Kali carbonicum shows marked aggravation on back, and may also wake early or feel unrefreshed.

rubrics.rubric: Dreams – of being pursued or chased
Dreams – vivid, can recall in detail

keyPoints.description: Stramonium and Belladonna show intense, frightening dreams – chased, murdered, animals, night terrors.
Phosphorus often has vivid, flying or erotic dreams, can recall in detail.
Lachesis dreams of snakes, symbolic or prophetic events, and sometimes erotic content.
Opium and Aconite show frightful dreams with sudden waking in terror.
Cannabis indica has vivid, colorful, symbolic and sometimes prophetic dreams.
Pulsatilla and Calcarea often have bland, thick white or yellowish discharges, non-excoriating.
Arsenicum, Mercurius and Sulphur show acrid, excoriating, and offensive discharges.
Kali bichromicum has thick, ropy, stringy, sticky and tenacious yellow discharges.
Sepia and Nitric acid are known for fishy-smelling or cheese-like offensive discharges.
Phosphorus and Kreosote have bloody, watery, or brown discharges, sometimes offensive.

rubrics.rubric: Chilly – aggravated by cold air or cold bath
Chilly – wants head and feet covered
Chilly – worse uncovering even a part
Hot – worse in sun, summer, warm room
Hot – desires open air, cool breeze
Hot – aggravated by warm food or drink

keyPoints.description: Silicea and Psorinum are extremely chilly – want full covering, worse uncovering, need warmth always.
Sulphur, Lachesis, and Pulsatilla are classic hot patients – worse from heat, crave open air, sleep uncovered.
Nux vomica and Ignatia show contradictions – chilly yet worse from heat or overheating.
Phosphorus shows changeable thermal state – hot but can be chilly, needs open air but worse from draft.
Calcarea carbonica is chilly but may sweat easily – especially on head, and wants warmth to sleep.

rubrics.rubric: Sensitive – to being looked at or spoken to

keyPoints.description: Nux vomica, Chamomilla, and Hepar are highly sensitive to touch and noise – irritable, angry type.
Belladonna shows extreme photophobia and noise sensitivity, especially in acute conditions.
Natrum mur and Phosphorus are emotionally sensitive – to music, odors, touch.
Lachesis cannot bear pressure – hates tight clothes or bands, especially around neck or abdomen.
Ignatia and Carcinosin are emotionally hypersensitive – dislike being looked at or spoken to.

rubrics.rubric: Aggravation from sudden change of weather

keyPoints.description: Rhus tox and Dulcamara are worse in cold damp weather; common in joint, back, and skin complaints.
Phosphorus and Silicea are weather-sensitive – worse before storms or during atmospheric changes.
Pulsatilla and Sulphur feel better in open air, while Nux vomica and Hepar are aggravated by it.
Natrum mur and Ignatia worsen in gloomy, cloudy, or overcast weather – affecting mood and energy.
Belladonna and Glonoinum are very heat-sensitive – aggravated in hot, dry sun and heat stroke-like states.

rubrics.rubric: Sweat – during sleep, but not when awake
Sweat – staining linen yellow or brown

keyPoints.description: Silicea and Calcarea carbonica sweat on head, feet, and at night – useful in children and weak constitutions.
Phosphorus and China show weakness from slight exertion with sweating – night sweats common.
Mercurius has offensive sweat that stains linen – with general weakness and salivation.
Veratrum and Carbo veg show cold, clammy sweat – especially during collapse or shock states.
Graphites and Thuja show sticky, oily sweat – often on genitals or flexures.
Nux vomica and Sulphur are typically worse in the early morning hours – irritable and sluggish.
Arsenicum and Kali carbonicum have aggravation after midnight, especially around 2–3 a.m.
Lycopodium has marked aggravation from 4–8 p.m., with gas, weakness, and irritability.
Pulsatilla and Rhus tox worsen in the evening and night, but Pulsatilla often improves in open air.
Sepia and Natrum mur feel better in the morning, worse in forenoon or evening.
Arsenicum has chill beginning in extremities, thirst, restlessness, and desire for warmth.
Gelsemium chills start in back, no thirst, with drowsiness and dullness.
Pulsatilla and Ignatia have chill or fever with no thirst – common in mild fevers.
Bryonia and Eupatorium have high fever with intense body pain and great thirst.
China and Nux vomica are suited in malarial fevers with chills and weakness after sweating.

rubrics.rubric: Aggravation after eating even small quantities
Aggravation after fatty or rich food
Aggravation after fruit or sour food
Aggravation after cold drinks or ice cream
Empty feeling in stomach not better by eating

keyPoints.description: Lycopodium has bloating, flatulence, and aggravation after small quantities – especially dinner.
Pulsatilla, Ant crudum, and Nux vomica aggravate after fatty, rich, or spicy foods.
Sepia and Calcarea show intolerance to milk and nausea after eating.
Phosphorus and Sulphur prefer cold drinks but are often aggravated by them.
Ignatia and Anacardium improve after eating – also indicated in emotional suppression with hunger pangs.
Pulsatilla is key in late, scanty, changeable menses with emotional sensitivity.
Sepia has irregular menses, bearing down pains, and emotional indifference.
Lachesis often has dark, offensive, profuse flow worse before menses.
Belladonna and Sabina suit bright red or clotty menses with cramping pain.
Silicea and Graphites help painful, delayed, or suppressed menstruation with weakness.
Kreosote is top for acrid, bloody, fishy, and green discharges that cause soreness.
Pulsatilla has bland, white, creamy, non-irritating discharges that often change character.
Sepia has thin, yellow-green, offensive or fishy discharges with marked itching.
Mercurius and Medorrhinum cover foul, excoriating, green or yellow discharge with odor.
Borax and Calcarea suit thick, white or sticky discharges in children and mild adults.
Stramonium often has vivid, fearful dreams of darkness, ghosts, and being pursued.
Lachesis and Phosphorus frequently dream of snakes, death, or sexual themes.
Silicea and Argentum-nitricum are key in performance anxiety dreams (exams, failure).
Sulphur and Pulsatilla often dream of falling or flying – symbolic of insecurity or freedom.
Ignatia and Natrum mur reflect suppressed grief in dreams of death or loved ones lost.

rubrics.rubric: Aggravation before or during a storm

keyPoints.description: Rhus tox and Dulcamara are top for aggravation in damp or rainy weather.
Natrum carbonicum and Phosphorus are highly sensitive to cloudy weather and light changes.
Belladonna, Nux vomica, and Glonoine react intensely to light, sound, or odors.
Pulsatilla is generally better in open air and worse in warm, stuffy rooms.
Argentum nitricum and Lycopodium dislike crowded places or anticipation of environmental triggers.

rubrics.rubric: Sleep – time – always wakes at same hour

keyPoints.description: Sulphur is a leading remedy across all sleep modalities: heat, position, waking time, unrefreshing sleep.
Nux-v and Ars are major remedies for insomnia, disturbed sleep after midnight, and irritability.
Coffea and Zincum cover difficulty falling asleep from mental activity or nervous excitation.
Phosphorus and Kali-carb commonly wake at 3 a.m. or same hour every night.

category: Discharge Modalities – Color, Odor, Consistency, Modalities

keyPoints.description: Pulsatilla and Calcarea are useful for thick, bland, white/yellow discharges without irritation.
Kreosote and Medorrhinum are strongly indicated for offensive, fishy, acrid discharges.
Kali-bichrome and Hydrastis for sticky, ropy, stringy discharges especially from nose or cervix.
Mercurius and Sulphur for excoriating, yellow-greenish, burning discharges.

rubrics.rubric: Menses – flow bright red and profuse
Menses – worse by standing or walking
Menses – better by bending forward or lying down

keyPoints.description: Sepia is a key remedy when symptoms are worse during or after menses, especially with pelvic heaviness.
Lachesis often has symptoms before or during menses, with intolerance to tight clothing and left-sided symptoms.
Pulsatilla is indicated for late, scanty, changeable flow, and better in open air.
Mag-phos and Colocynthis are top remedies for painful menses relieved by heat or pressure.

rubrics.rubric: General – worse from touch (even light touch)
General – worse from jar, stepping, concussion
General – worse lying on painful side
General – better lying on painful side

keyPoints.description: Pulsatilla is a keynote for improvement in open air and intolerance to warm, closed rooms.
Colocynth and Mag-phos show marked relief from pressure and bending double in colic or neuralgic pains.
Lachesis is highly sensitive to touch and tight clothing, with worse from left side lying.
Hepar and Silicea are chilly, worse from uncovering or slightest exposure to cold.
